summary: Listener is preparing for the Maryland June 23, 2026 Democratic primary and wants candidate-specific, life-history-deep coverage — not just recent session votes. Strong recurring asks: sanctuary policy, Andrew Friedson's fiscal record, Wootton High School closure, and a side-by-side comparison of every Democratic primary candidate on the listener's ballot.
themes: Career-long historical research on candidates | Listener has repeatedly asked for old news, public records, and career arc — not just recent voting roll calls.
Concrete side-by-side policy comparison across primary ballot | Listener wants to know what differentiates the candidates on each race.
Sanctuary policy on illegal immigration | Listener has asked which Democratic primary candidates oppose sanctuary frameworks — needs candidate-by-candidate answer.
Fiscal restraint / spending discipline as a campaign axis | Specifically anchored to Andrew Friedson and the MoCo Executive race.
MCPS Wootton High School closure | Listener wants to know why, what happens next, and which candidates have positions on it.
open_questions: Who among Democratic primary candidates actually opposes sanctuary policy on illegal immigration?
Is Andrew Friedson the fiscal restriction / spending discipline candidate in the MoCo Executive race?
Why does MCPS want to close and move Wootton High School, and what happens next?
What is Ben Kramer's full historical voting record, including old news and public records?
What are the documented policy differences among every candidate on the listener's primary ballot?
underserved_topics: Federal-employee constituency issues in MD-8 (pay, RIFs, telework, agency relocations) — barely surfaced in episodes to date
Maryland Board of Education / MCPS school-board race coverage
Property tax dynamics in Montgomery County and what the County Council vs County Executive actually control
Circuit Court Judge 6th Judicial Circuit race (no episodes yet)
avoid_list: The framing 'a listener asked us last week/earlier this week which Democratic primary candidates actually oppose sanctuary policy on illegal immigration' as a setup line — overused
The Evan Glass DHS-screening bill as the concrete sanctuary-policy example — already used twice
The ICE mask-ban ordinance as the sanctuary-policy benchmark — already used
'Two stories, properly worked through' / 'two stories, not spreading thin across six' framing — already used
Governor Moore's 'Leave No One Behind' slogan vs disability service cuts — already used twice
The ACA marketplace quiet-downgrade story for Marylanders — already used twice
Maryland's 90-day part-time legislature explanation — already used twice
The wave of 25 Maryland lawmakers retiring or running for other offices framing — already used three times
Beltsville Agricultural Research Center closure as a District 19 candidate-record hook — already used twice
Narrating the listener's own frustration about promised deep-dives back to them — already used in 5+ episodes
